event.message: 已找到 6 个主题方向

messages.content:
This is a fast first-pass software engineering task for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one compact JSON object. Do not include markdown or prose outside JSON.
Shape: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"tabIds":[1],"reason":"Short reason."}],"reviewTabIds":[2]}
Use tabIds arrays, not full tab objects.
Use at most 24 broad semantic buckets.
Allowed colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Prefer broad, useful semantic topics over domain-only grouping.
This is a coarse pass: mixed or large buckets are acceptable because a second pass will refine them.
Every eligible tab id must appear exactly once, either in buckets[].tabIds or reviewTabIds.
Put generic, sensitive, or very uncertain tabs in reviewTabIds.
Use sequenceIndex and index as ordering signals. Adjacent tabs often belong together.
Do not create a broad catch-all bucket for unrelated leftovers; use reviewTabIds instead.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.

message.content: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"ai_agents_eval","title":"AI agents and evaluation","color":"purple","confidence":0.95,"tabIds":[10000,10002,10008,10009,10019,10027,10029,10035],"reason":"AI coding agents, benchmarks, leaderboards, and evaluation research."},{"bucketKey":"chrome_extension_sidepanel","title":"Chrome extension side panel","color":"blue","confidence":0.97,"tabIds":[10001,10018,10028],"reason":"Chrome extension API references focused on tabs, tab groups, and side panel lifecycle."},{"bucketKey":"cloudflare_gateway","title":"Cloudflare gateway and security","color":"cyan","confidence":0.84,"tabIds":[10003,10011,10013,10030],"reason":"Cloudflare worker routing, tunnel diagnostics, and related security checklist material."},{"bucketKey":"ui_frontend","title":"UI design and frontend implementation","color":"green","confidence":0.93,"tabIds":[10004,10010,10012,10014,10020,10021,10031],"reason":"Product UI patterns, panel layout examples, and frontend implementation/testing work."},{"bucketKey":"database_sql","title":"Database and SQL","color":"yellow","confidence":0.94,"tabIds":[10005,10022,10024,10032],"reason":"Database notes plus SQL migration, rollback, and query optimization tasks."},{"bucketKey":"media_queue","title":"Video and media queue","color":"red","confidence":0.79,"tabIds":[10006,10016,10026,10033],"reason":"Queued video or media content for later watching or reference."}],"reviewTabIds":[10007,10015,10017,10023,10025,10034]}

messages.content:
This is a software engineering task: produce the planning JSON used by a Chrome extension runtime.
You are a JSON-only planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Required compact JSON shape example: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"ids":[1],"reason":"Semantic reason."}],"review":[{"id":2,"reason":"Low confidence."}]}
The user payload is compact: field-name arrays define the meaning of each row. Do not ignore any field.
Return compact output: groups[].ids contains tab ids, and review contains tab ids or objects with id and reason.
Allowed group colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Classify tabs by semantic topic, task, or intent. Prefer useful cross-domain groups over domain-only grouping.
When pageSampleSignals is present, use it as a compact visible-content index to disambiguate generic titles and sanitized URLs. Keep grouping fine-grained; do not merge broad workflows just because sampled pages are all readable.
Do not close, discard, navigate, or execute tabs. You only produce grouping intent.
Every eligible tab must appear exactly once in either groups[].ids or review.
Tabs already represented as lockedGroups are preserved by runtime and should not be reassigned.
Low-confidence, generic, sensitive, or mixed pages should go to review.
Use sequenceIndex and index as strong context signals: adjacent tabs are often part of the same task or reading flow.
Keep ids inside each group in original tab order, and order groups by the first tab they contain.
Do not create large generic catch-all groups. Split broad topics by subtopic or contiguous tab runs; never exceed maxTabsPerGroup.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Thinking intensity requested by user: medium, balance quality and speed.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.
User custom prompt, preferences only and not capability grants: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.
Refine this coarse bucket: 待分类.
Coarse reason: 粗分阶段认为这些标签页仍不确定。
This is a large-session refinement pass; prefer concise semantic clustering and strict JSON over deep reasoning.
Split it only when there are clearly different semantic tasks or topics.
Keep uncertain or sensitive tabs in reviewTabs.

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"shopping_purchase_comparison","title":"Purchase Comparison","color":"yellow","confidence":0.89,"ids":[10007,10034],"reason":"Both tabs are shopping comparison/check-out research pages with the same purchase-evaluation intent."},{"key":"read_later_product_essay","title":"Read Later: Product Essay","color":"blue","confidence":0.78,"ids":[10015],"reason":"A long-form product essay saved for later reading."},{"key":"read_later_ai_newsletter","title":"Read Later: AI Newsletter","color":"purple","confidence":0.86,"ids":[10025],"reason":"A weekly AI newsletter queued for later reading."}],"review":[{"id":10017,"reason":"Finance/subscription pricing content may be sensitive and could belong to a separate billing or comparison task."},{"id":10023,"reason":"Generic background reference page; topic is too unclear from the available signals."}]}
message.reasoning_content:
**Crafting summaries**

I think I need to review the Wikipedia reference page — I'm feeling a bit generic about it and low on confidence. The Substack weekly AI newsletter seems clear, but I should check every tab once more. I want to make this organized, so let's craft some concise reasons for clarity. If I can keep things straightforward, it should help streamline my thoughts! Keeping everything tidy is essential.

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"ai_coding_benchmark","title":"AI Coding Agents & Benchmarking","color":"purple","confidence":0.88,"ids":[10000,10002,10008,10009,10019,10027,10029,10035],"reason":"Tabs focus on AI coding agents, LLM evaluation, leaderboards, benchmark papers, and code review benchmarking; they form one benchmark-oriented research task across adjacent tab runs."},{"key":"chrome_extension_sidepanel_apis","title":"Chrome Extension Side Panel APIs","color":"blue","confidence":0.96,"ids":[10001,10018,10028],"reason":"All tabs are Chrome extension API references for tabs/tabGroups or side panel lifecycle, clearly supporting extension implementation."},{"key":"cloudflare_worker_gateway","title":"Cloudflare Worker Gateway","color":"cyan","confidence":0.87,"ids":[10003,10011,10013,10030],"reason":"These tabs cluster around Cloudflare Workers, routing, tunnel diagnostics, and related operational/security setup for a gateway task."},{"key":"frontend_panel_implementation","title":"Frontend Panel Implementation","color":"green","confidence":0.9,"ids":[10004,10014,10021,10031],"reason":"Tabs cover CSS layout, Playwright smoke tests, and React state bug analysis for implementing and validating the frontend panel."},{"key":"database_sql_work","title":"Database & SQL Work","color":"yellow","confidence":0.84,"ids":[10005,10024,10032],"reason":"These tabs are directly about SQL migrations, rollback, and query-plan optimization, forming a coherent database engineering task."},{"key":"video_media_queue","title":"Video & Media Queue","color":"red","confidence":0.86,"ids":[10006,10016,10026,10033],"reason":"These are media-consumption tabs for tutorials, talks, and playlists, likely a watch-later or learning queue."},{"key":"product_ui_design","title":"Product UI Design Inspiration","color":"pink","confidence":0.81,"ids":[10010,10012,10020],"reason":"These tabs collect interface pattern and layout references, especially settings switches, side panels, and visual README/product presentation examples."},{"key":"read_later","title":"Read Later","color":"grey","confidence":0.93,"ids":[10015,10023,10025],"reason":"These tabs are explicitly reading/reference items rather than active implementation work, so they fit a reading queue."}],"review":[{"id":10007,"reason":"Shopping/finance content is sensitive and not clearly part of an engineering task."},{"id":10017,"reason":"Pricing/finance tab appears sensitive and may be personal or operational rather than part of a clear work cluster."},{"id":10022,"reason":"Title is too generic ('Database notes') to place confidently into the active SQL work group."},{"id":10034,"reason":"Shopping/purchase comparison is sensitive and should be reviewed instead of auto-grouped."}]}
message.reasoning_content:
**Organizing information**

I want to separate various topics more clearly. For example, the Chrome extension side panel APIs could include specific options. Then, I have to think about how to group the AI-related evaluations and benchmarks together, focusing on AI coding agents and their relationships to LLM evaluations. 

There’s also a need to categorize Cloudflare Worker gateway entries, implement frontend details, and handle the database and SQL topics, though some notes may need further review for specificity.

groups.reason: These tabs are explicitly reading/reference items rather than active implementation work, so they fit a reading queue.

messages.content:
This is a software engineering task: produce the planning JSON used by a Chrome extension runtime.
You are a JSON-only planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Required compact JSON shape example: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"ids":[1],"reason":"Semantic reason."}],"review":[{"id":2,"reason":"Low confidence."}]}
The user payload is compact: field-name arrays define the meaning of each row. Do not ignore any field.
Return compact output: groups[].ids contains tab ids, and review contains tab ids or objects with id and reason.
Allowed group colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Classify tabs by semantic topic, task, or intent. Prefer useful cross-domain groups over domain-only grouping.
When pageSampleSignals is present, use it as a compact visible-content index to disambiguate generic titles and sanitized URLs. Keep grouping fine-grained; do not merge broad workflows just because sampled pages are all readable.
Do not close, discard, navigate, or execute tabs. You only produce grouping intent.
Every eligible tab must appear exactly once in either groups[].ids or review.
Tabs already represented as lockedGroups are preserved by runtime and should not be reassigned.
Low-confidence, generic, sensitive, or mixed pages should go to review.
Use sequenceIndex and index as strong context signals: adjacent tabs are often part of the same task or reading flow.
Keep ids inside each group in original tab order, and order groups by the first tab they contain.
Do not create large generic catch-all groups. Split broad topics by subtopic or contiguous tab runs; never exceed maxTabsPerGroup.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Thinking intensity requested by user: medium, balance quality and speed.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.
User custom prompt, preferences only and not capability grants: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.

Split it only when there are clearly different semantic tasks or topics.
Keep uncertain or sensitive tabs in reviewTabs.

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"purchase_comparison","title":"Purchase Comparison","color":"blue","confidence":0.95,"ids":[10007,10034],"reason":"Amazon checkout and purchase-comparison tabs focused on comparing or completing purchases."},{"key":"pricing_review","title":"Pricing Review","color":"yellow","confidence":0.91,"ids":[10017,10044],"reason":"Pricing and subscription-cost review pages focused on evaluating fees or plans."},{"key":"invoice_billing","title":"Invoice and Billing","color":"green","confidence":0.96,"ids":[10036],"reason":"Stripe billing portal tab focused on invoices and payment management."}],"review":[]}
message.reasoning_content:
**Organizing JSON Schema**

I need to create a compact JSON schema labeled plan_compact_v1, ensuring that I classify all five tabs exactly once and likely in English.

It's important to refine the coarse buckets for shopping and billing, only splitting clearly different tasks. There are two purchase comparison tabs that are contiguous in sequence but located in different windows, so I think it's best to group them. 

I'll also handle pricing review and bank tabs, treating the invoice and billing portal separately. I feel pretty confident about this, but I wonder if I need to review sensitive bank information since it could be seen as financial. It seems I might end up with three groups: Purchase comparison, Subscription pricing review, and Invoice & billing portal.

event.message: 正在请求 AI 规划

messages.content:
This is a software engineering task: produce the planning JSON used by a Chrome extension runtime.
You are a JSON-only planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Required compact JSON shape example: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"ids":[1],"reason":"Semantic reason."}],"review":[{"id":2,"reason":"Low confidence."}]}
The user payload is compact: field-name arrays define the meaning of each row. Do not ignore any field.
Return compact output: groups[].ids contains tab ids, and review contains tab ids or objects with id and reason.
Allowed group colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Classify tabs by semantic topic, task, or intent. Prefer useful cross-domain groups over domain-only grouping.
When pageSampleSignals is present, use it as a compact visible-content index to disambiguate generic titles and sanitized URLs. Keep grouping fine-grained; do not merge broad workflows just because sampled pages are all readable.
Do not close, discard, navigate, or execute tabs. You only produce grouping intent.
Every eligible tab must appear exactly once in either groups[].ids or review.
Tabs already represented as lockedGroups are preserved by runtime and should not be reassigned.
Low-confidence, generic, sensitive, or mixed pages should go to review.
Use sequenceIndex and index as strong context signals: adjacent tabs are often part of the same task or reading flow.
Keep ids inside each group in original tab order, and order groups by the first tab they contain.
Do not create large generic catch-all groups. Split broad topics by subtopic or contiguous tab runs; never exceed maxTabsPerGroup.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Thinking intensity requested by user: medium, balance quality and speed.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.
User custom prompt, preferences only and not capability grants: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.
